description.content: Operations for analyzing existing images, including the describe endpoint that generates text prompts from uploaded images.

item.name: Generate text prompts from an image

request.name: Generate text prompts from an image

description.content: Analyzes an uploaded image and generates multiple text prompt suggestions that could be used to recreate a similar image with the imagine endpoint. The image can be provided as a URL or base64-encoded data. Returns up to four prompt suggestions per image.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Invalid request. The image URL may be inaccessible or the base64 data malformed.

Operations for generating images from text prompts, including the core imagine endpoint that produces a grid of images from a natural language description.

item.name: Generate images from a text prompt

request.name: Generate images from a text prompt

description.content: Submits a text prompt to generate a grid of up to four unique images using Midjourney's AI models. The request is processed asynchronously and returns a job identifier that can be used to poll for results or receive updates via webhook. Supports parameters for aspect ratio, model version, quality, stylization, and other generation settings embedded in the prompt string or as separate parameters.

response.name: Job successfully created. Returns a job identifier for tracking the asynchronous image generation process.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Invalid request. The prompt may be empty, contain blocked content, or parameters may be malformed.

Operations for manipulating existing generated images, including upscaling to higher resolutions, creating variations, blending multiple images, and region-based inpainting.

item.name: Upscale a generated image to higher resolution

request.name: Upscale a generated image to higher resolution

description.content: Upscales a specific image from a previously generated image grid to a higher resolution. Requires the job identifier of the original generation and the index of the image to upscale (1-4). The upscaled image is produced asynchronously and the result can be retrieved via the job status endpoint or webhook callback.

response.name: Upscale job successfully created. Returns a new job identifier for tracking the upscale operation.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Invalid request. The parent job ID may be invalid or the image index out of range.

item.name: Create variations of a generated image

request.name: Create variations of a generated image

description.content: Generates a new grid of image variations based on a specific image from a previously generated grid. Requires the job identifier of the original generation and the index of the image to vary (1-4). Each variation maintains the general style and composition of the source image while introducing creative differences.

Blends two to five images together to create a new composite image that merges the visual concepts, styles, and compositions of the source images. Images can be provided as URLs or base64-encoded data. An optional text prompt can be included to guide the blending process.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Invalid request. At least two images are required and no more than five are allowed.

Operations for tracking and managing asynchronous image generation jobs, including retrieving job status, results, and listing previous jobs.

item.name: Get the status and results of a job

request.name: Get the status and results of a job

description.content: Retrieves the current status, progress, and results of an asynchronous image generation job. Returns the job metadata including status, progress percentage, and when complete, the URLs of the generated images. Poll this endpoint to track job progress when webhooks are not configured.

Retrieves a paginated list of image generation jobs for the authenticated account. Jobs can be filtered by status and sorted by creation date. Returns job metadata including status, prompts, and result URLs for completed jobs.

item.name: Cancel a pending or in-progress job

request.name: Cancel a pending or in-progress job

description.content: Cancels a job that is currently pending or in progress. Jobs that have already completed or failed cannot be cancelled. Cancelled jobs do not consume generation credits.

The Midjourney Image Generation API provides programmatic access to Midjourney's AI-powered image generation capabilities. Developers can submit text prompts to generate images, upscale selected outputs to higher resolutions, create variations of generated images, describe existing images to generate prompts, and blend multiple images together. The API uses an asynchronous job-based workflow where image generation requests return a job identifier that can be polled for status and results. Webhook callbacks are supported for real-time job status notifications. Enterprise API access is available through the Midjourney Enterprise dashboard.
